title: Bergamot and neroli room spray
subtitle: A bright, floral-citrus room spray with bergamot and neroli essential oils in an alcohol and water base
excerpt: Bergamot and neroli is a classic perfumery pairing: bergamot brings fresh citrus top notes and neroli brings white-floral depth. This room spray uses isopropyl alcohol to disperse the essential oils in water; without it the oils would float as droplets rather than misting evenly. Shake before each use.
sourceNotes: Room spray formulation. Isopropyl alcohol (IPA) at 99% as solubiliser and quick-evaporating base: 20 to 25% of total. Distilled water at 73 to 78% of total. Essential oils at 2% total. Bergamot (Citrus bergamia): standard bergapten-free version preferred for room sprays to avoid skin photosensitivity risk if mist lands on skin. Neroli (Citrus aurantium var. amara, flowers): expensive; substitute with neroli fragrance oil for cost efficiency. Shake before each use.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep IPA at 20 to 25% to maintain adequate dispersion.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a spray bottle. Citrus top notes fade fastest; the neroli base note persists longer. Keep away from direct sunlight.

glossaryTerms.definition: The most volatile fragrance compounds, perceived immediately when a scent is first encountered. Bergamot is a top note: bright and instantly noticeable, but the first to fade as the spray dries.
The least volatile fragrance compounds, which provide depth and longevity to a blend. Neroli has strong base-note character in a room spray: it lingers after the bergamot citrus has lifted.

A small amount of isopropyl or ethyl alcohol added to a water-based spray to disperse essential oils and prevent them floating as visible droplets. Without it, oils and water separate and the spray mists unevenly.

that disperses the oils in water so the spray mists as a fine even cloud rather than separate droplets. Isopropyl alcohol is flammable; keep away from open flames while mixing and using.

items.prepNote: 99% IPA; add to bottle first before water
Distilled or freshly boiled and cooled
Or neroli fragrance oil; base note

content.text: Add the isopropyl alcohol to the spray bottle first. Add the essential oils to the alcohol and swirl to mix. Add the distilled water. Cap and shake for 30 seconds. Label with the contents and date.
Shake before every use. Mist into the room from about 60 cm away, or spray lightly onto curtains and soft furnishings. Avoid misting directly onto polished wood surfaces.
6 months in a sealed spray bottle away from sunlight. Shake before each use.
